rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Use component type annexes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Use implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type annexes when concrete bounds are available
generic_rules: Insert annex blocks in the intended target component or implementation scope
Preserve existing AADL architecture by not modifying component types or implementations beyond annex insertion
Use visible identifiers when available to maintain traceability and avoid unresolved references
Avoid using implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
For output dependency contracts, relate visible outputs to visible inputs in component type annexes
anti_patterns: Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through variables or outputs
Do not place assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports in invalid scopes
